pi_items.source: Cross-verified in task.json description (line 5), setup_task.sh (line 9), and export_result.sh (line 9). All files reference identical path.
Task description: 'Draw a 300mm x 300mm square centered at the origin (0,0)'. Verifier.py line 193 checks for width/height within ±5mm of 300.0mm.

Task description: 'Draw a circle with diameter 150mm (radius 75mm) centered at (0,0)'. Verifier.py line 89 checks for radius within ±2mm of 75.0mm. Specification is mathematically correct: 150mm diameter / 2 = 75mm radius.

Task description: 'Draw 8 bolt holes (diameter 12mm / radius 6mm each)'. Verifier.py line 108 checks for radius within ±1mm of 6.0mm. Specification is mathematically correct: 12mm diameter / 2 = 6mm radius.

Task description: 'on a 230mm diameter (115mm radius) bolt circle centered at (0,0)'. Verifier.py line 128 checks for average bolt hole radial distance within ±3mm of 115.0mm. Specification is mathematically correct: 230mm diameter / 2 = 115mm radius.

Task description: 'Draw 8 bolt holes (diameter 12mm / radius 6mm each) evenly spaced on a 230mm diameter bolt circle' with 'Holes are spaced 45 degrees apart (0, 45, 90, 135, 180, 225, 270, 315)'. Verifier.py line 110 checks for exactly 8 bolt holes.

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic CAD task where a user must create a 2D technical drawing of a bolt circle plate in LibreCAD. The plate specifications are: a 300×300 mm square centered at origin with a central circular opening (radius 75 mm) and 8 equally-spaced bolt holes (radius 6 mm each) positioned on a bolt circle of radius 115 mm, with the first hole at 0° (position 115, 0). The holes are spaced 45° apart around the circle. Output must be saved as a DXF file to /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/bolt_circle_plate.dxf. All metadata values are internally consistent with the task description and verifier logic.
